components.name: PostgreSQL Database
components.description: Stores users and URL references to audio and images stored on Cloudinary

components.name: Express Server
components.description: Fetches data from PostgreSQL database using Sequelize, serves React frontend, JWT token validation

components.name: Cloudinary
components.description: Stores audio and generates waveform visuals, stores images, sends back URLs

components.name: React Frontend
components.description: Redux for state management, actions upload images and audio to Cloudinary, app component controls playback of audio, sound component visualizes playback

connectors.label: Frontend makes Fetch calls to backend to store user and sound data
Frontend sends audio and image files
Cloudinary sends back URLs leading to files

components.name: Frontend
components.description: Contains components such as Home.js, Dashboard.js, SoundDetail.js, etc.

components.name: Backend
components.description: Node.js Server with Express API, connects to PostgreSQL Database and Cloudinary API

components.name: Redux
components.description: Redux Store with configureStore.js, redux-thunk, Reducers, Actions

components.name: External Services
components.description: External services used by the application

comparison.explanation: The GroundTruth diagram provides a high-level overview of the Soundzone application architecture, focusing on major components like the PostgreSQL Database, Express Server, Cloudinary, and React Frontend. It highlights the interactions between these components, particularly the flow of data between the frontend and backend. In contrast, the GeneratedImage diagram offers a more detailed breakdown of the frontend and backend, including specific files and modules within the frontend and Redux store. The GeneratedImage lacks explicit mention of the React Frontend as a distinct component but includes a broader 'Frontend' group. Common elements include the PostgreSQL Database and Cloudinary, but the GeneratedImage introduces an 'External Services' component not present in the GroundTruth. The GroundTruth emphasizes the flow of data and interactions, while the GeneratedImage provides a more granular view of the application's structure. Rating: 7/10. The GeneratedImage captures the essential components and their interactions but lacks some of the specific details and clarity found in the GroundTruth, particularly regarding the React Frontend.
